mood_status.text: Your mood has been trending upward this week — that's a positive sign worth acknowledging.
Your mood has been lower than usual lately. It is okay to have harder stretches — be gentle with yourself.
Your mood has dipped a little over the past few days. Keep tracking — patterns help identify what helps.
Your mood has been steady and fairly positive over the past week. Nice to see that consistency.
You've been checking in consistently, which is the most important habit for understanding your wellbeing.
There's been noticeable variability in your mood this week — that's worth paying attention to.
Your mood has been recovering after a rough patch — that upward movement is encouraging.
You've had a strong stretch of positive moods recently. Keep building on that momentum.

health_context.text: Some of your active symptoms may warrant a professional check-in — consider reaching out to a healthcare provider soon.
Having multiple active symptoms while your mood is dipping can feel draining. Rest and hydration are a good starting point.
You have active symptoms to keep monitoring. Note any changes and consult a professional if things worsen.
Your fitness trend is heading upward — physical momentum like this often carries over into energy and mood.
Both your fitness and mood have been declining — even a short walk or rest day can help break that cycle.
Your fitness level has been consistent, which provides a stable base to build on.
No active symptoms and stable fitness — your physical health looks solid this week.
Low activity combined with low mood can reinforce each other — even light movement can help shift that.

actionable_closing.text: Try to prioritize one small act of self-care today — movement, sleep, or connection — and see what shifts.
Your mood has been variable this week. Identifying triggers in your logs can help bring more consistency.
Keep logging — every entry helps build a clearer picture of your health.
You are doing the right thing by staying consistent with your check-ins.
Small, consistent actions add up. Keep building on what you track here.
Rest today and keep the load light — recovery matters just as much as effort.
A short walk or low-intensity activity today could help both your mood and energy levels.
Consider reaching out to someone you trust — connection is one of the fastest mood regulators.
